name: Conduktor Kafka Management API
what_it_does: Kafka management and governance platform with REST API for managing topics, consumer groups, schemas, and access control across multiple Kafka clusters from a central control plane.
use_cases: Managing Kafka topics, consumer groups, and ACLs programmatically via REST API
Monitoring consumer lag and throughput metrics for alerting agents
Enforcing data quality policies on Kafka messages via Conduktor Gateway
Managing schema registry schemas and compatibility across Kafka clusters
Automating Kafka cluster governance including topic creation and quota enforcement
not_for: Direct Kafka message production/consumption — use native Kafka clients instead
Organizations already using Confluent Cloud with built-in management tools
Simple single-cluster Kafka deployments without governance requirements
best_when: An agent manages multi-cluster Kafka governance, enforces topic standards, and monitors consumer health across multiple teams and environments.
avoid_when: You use Confluent Cloud — Confluent's native management tools are sufficient.

auth.notes: API key or JWT bearer token from Conduktor Console. Role-based access control with Admin, Developer, and custom roles. Per-cluster and per-topic permissions available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Conduktor Desktop: free for local development; Console: free tier for 1 cluster
pricing.paid_tiers: Console Cloud from $250/month; Enterprise on request
pricing.notes: Conduktor Desktop is free. Console (SaaS) has free tier for single cluster. Enterprise version adds governance, compliance, and multi-cluster management at scale.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation available but less comprehensive than Confluent. Error responses are JSON. Consumer lag metrics and health endpoints are useful for monitoring agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Topic creation is idempotent — creating existing topic returns success. ACL and schema operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-managed Kafka requires Conduktor Console to connect to your Kafka clusters first
Gateway (data quality/masking layer) is separate from Console — different API surface
Consumer lag data has polling delay — not real-time streaming of metrics
Multi-cluster management requires separate cluster registrations with credentials

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 and GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. RBAC for multi-team Kafka governance. Conduktor Gateway adds data masking and encryption at Kafka broker level.
